summary: Tested new city data seeding for 5 cities (Mumbai, Bangalore, Hyderabad, Chennai, Chandigarh). All 36 backend tests pass. All frontend flows verified end-to-end. Found and fixed a CRITICAL bug: new venues had amenities stored as list of strings instead of VenueAmenities dict format, causing GET /api/venues?city=* to return 500 for all new cities. Fixed by running /app/backend/scripts/fix_amenities_format.py which converted 20 venues' amenities to correct dict format.

critical.issue: 500 Internal Server Error - new seeded venues stored amenities as list ['parking','valet',...] but VenueResponse model expects VenueAmenities dict. FIXED by running fix_amenities_format.py which converted all 20 new venues to correct format.

integration_issues.issue: URL /venues/city/mumbai matches route /venues/:citySlug/:venueSlug with citySlug='city' and venueSlug='mumbai', rendering VenuePublicPage and showing 'Venue not found'. The correct URL for Mumbai city page is /venues/mumbai. The review request listed /venues/city/mumbai as expected URL but actual routing doesn't support it.
integration_issues.severity: MINOR - not a regression; this URL pattern was never supported. The correct URL /venues/mumbai works perfectly.

action_items: Note: The seed script /app/backend/scripts/seed_new_cities.py stores amenities as a list of strings. Future re-runs of the seed script will cause the same 500 errors. E1 should either (a) update seed_new_cities.py to store amenities in VenueAmenities dict format OR (b) also run fix_amenities_format.py after seeding.

Optional: Add /venues/city/:citySlug route in App.js if the /venues/city/mumbai URL pattern is desired for SEO (currently the backend has /api/venues/city/:slug but the frontend doesn't have a matching client-side route).
critical_code_review_comments: seed_new_cities.py uses amenities as list=['parking','valet',...] which is incompatible with VenueAmenities Pydantic model. The correct format is a dict with bool fields. The fix script (fix_amenities_format.py) was applied to patch the live DB data.
The /api/venues search endpoint uses VenueResponse model which validates amenities as VenueAmenities dict. Any future seed scripts must use the correct dict format to avoid 500 errors.
seed_data_creation: 20 venues across 5 cities (Mumbai 4, Bangalore 4, Hyderabad 4, Chennai 4, Chandigarh 4) seeded by E1. Amenities format fixed by fix_amenities_format.py in this iteration.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 6 target cities (Delhi 11, Mumbai 4, Bangalore 4, Hyderabad 4, Chennai 4, Chandigarh 4) have seeded venues. The amenities format bug was fixed. All city pages work at /venues/{city_slug} (e.g. /venues/mumbai, /venues/bangalore). The /venues/city/{city_slug} URL pattern does NOT work (routes to wrong component). Search API /api/venues?city=* now returns all new venues correctly. Featured venues section shows: Taj Mahal Palace Mumbai (4.9), The Oberoi New Delhi (4.9), The Leela Palace New Delhi (4.9), The Imperial New Delhi (4.9). Price estimator works for all 6 cities.

backend_tests_passed: GET /api/venues/cities → 6 target cities all present, each with venue_count > 0 ✓
frontend_tests_passed: Browse by City: all 6 target cities visible with correct venue counts ✓
Clicking Mumbai city card → navigates to /venues/search?city=Mumbai ✓
/venues/mumbai → city page with 4 venues, Maharashtra state, area tags ✓
/venues/bangalore → city page with 4 venues, Karnataka state ✓
Price estimator: Mumbai/Wedding/200 shows price range (4 venues) ✓
Search bar city dropdown includes Mumbai, Bangalore, Hyderabad, Chennai, Chandigarh ✓
Featured venues: Taj Mahal Palace Mumbai (4.9) now in top 4 ✓
